Public record · open reading room · DATP draft

The plan, read in one place
with a public comment beneath it

The City of Colorado Springs has opened public comment on its 2026 Digital Accessibility Transition Plan. The plan itself is a 65-page PDF; this page lets you read it inline or download it, and lays a structured twelve-item comment beneath. Submit your own comment to the City directly using the addresses at the bottom.

12
Comments
65
Plan pages
Jan 26
Comment period ends
Apr 26
DOJ rule deadline
About this reading room. This is a courtesy mirror. The plan PDF is hosted by the City at coloradosprings.gov; public comment is collected by the City's Office of Accessibility, not here. The twelve items below are a single submitter's structured comment, drafted to be read alongside the plan. This page is not the official record — it is a reading aid intended to make the document and one set of comments easier to compare side by side. To submit your own comment, use the Office of Accessibility contact further down the page.

Step one

Read the plan itself

Open the document below to read the plan inline, or download the PDF if you'd rather read offline or with your own assistive technology. The PDF is tagged, has a structure tree, and declares English as its language; the title-bar metadata is empty, which is a minor issue worth noting.

› Digital Accessibility Transition Plan (Draft, 2026) PDF · 65 pages · ~0.8 MB · tagged · lang=EN-US Open viewer Close viewer
↓ Download PDF ↗ Open on coloradosprings.gov Last updated by City: Dec 8, 2025

Step two

Twelve structured comments

Each comment cites the page it addresses, paraphrases what the plan currently says, states the concern, and proposes a specific revision. The intent is for City staff to be able to address each item as a discrete revision request.

Submit your own comment

City of Colorado Springs · Office of Accessibility

The comment period on the Draft Digital Accessibility Transition Plan is open through the end of January 2026. Submit feedback through any of the channels below — the City has committed to providing all feedback to the Web Accessibility Coordinator and the Accessibility Prioritization Committee.

Email

ADAcompliance@ColoradoSprings.gov

Preferred for written comments and attachments.

Phone

719-385-5175

Dial 7-1-1 for Colorado Relay.

Mail

Office of Accessibility
City of Colorado Springs
PO Box 1575, Mail Code 325
Colorado Springs, CO 80901-1575

About this reading room

A residents' courtesy mirror of the City of Colorado Springs' 2026 Digital Accessibility Transition Plan and one set of structured public comments. Built so the document and one critique can be read side by side. Not the official record.

Source: DATP PDF · Accessible Information Technology page
